articles.title: EVA-Bench Data 2.0: 3 Domains, 121 Tools, 213 Scenarios
The Download: AI-generated lawsuits and virtual power plants for data centers
articles.summary: This is today's edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what's going on in the world of technology.

articles.title: Task-Seeded Synthetic Q&A Generation for Nemotron Pretraining
How courts are coping with a flood of AI-generated lawsuits
articles.summary: Most days in her chambers, Judge Maritza Braswell, a federal magistrate judge in Colorado, sifts through stacks of documents written by people without a lawyer.

articles.title: Lovable signs multiyear deal with Google Cloud to up usage 5x, source says
articles.summary: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.

articles.title: Alphabet's record-breaking $85B raise for Google's AI business is a helluva good signal
articles.summary: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.

articles.title: Google's new open source Gemma 4 12B analyzes audio, video — and runs entirely locally on a typical 16GB enterprise laptop
articles.summary: While many AI open source model providers are pursuing larger and more powerful models, Google is still giving attention to the smaller, more local side of the market.

articles.title: Microsoft Declares AI Independence With In-House Models
articles.summary: Microsoft announces its own AI models, breaking from dependence on OpenAI at Build 2026.

articles.title: Enterprise AI agents keep creating data silos. Microsoft's Build answer is Microsoft IQ and Rayfin.
articles.summary: Every new AI agent your team deploys starts from scratch: no memory of how the business works, where data lives, or what rules apply.

articles.title: Microsoft launches MXC, an OS-level sandbox for AI agents, with OpenAI and Nvidia already on board
articles.summary: On Tuesday at its annual Build developer conference, Microsoft offered what may be the industry's most serious answer yet to AI agent security.

articles.title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
articles.summary: Coralogix is among a growing number of infrastructure firms betting that as AI systems move into production, demand will rise for tools that can monitor their behavior.

articles.title: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
articles.summary: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage.

articles.title: OpenAI's Codex update lets agents build interactive enterprise workspaces via Sites and role-specific plugins
articles.summary: Agentic AI is moving rapidly from the developer terminal to the corporate world.

articles.title: Microsoft debuts Surface RTX Spark Dev Box to run large AI models without cloud costs
articles.summary: Microsoft unveiled the Surface RTX Spark Dev Box, a compact desktop computer designed to let software developers run large AI models on their desks instead of paying for cloud computing.

articles.title: How will AI sycophancy change us? Early signs are not encouraging
articles.summary: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis, warns Box co-founder Aaron Levie, as AI sycophancy emerges as an enterprise risk.

articles.title: Perplexity AI unveils hybrid local-cloud inference system at Computex 2026
articles.summary: Perplexity AI unveiled software that autonomously decides in real time which AI workloads stay on a user's device and which get routed to frontier models in the cloud.

articles.title: AI alone won't change your business. The system running it will.
articles.summary: AI has arrived in the enterprise, and the shift is happening all at once.

articles.title: Ideas: slow down to speed up when working with AI agents
articles.summary: Devs are generating twice as much code (or more) than just 6 months ago, which is a problem for quality, reliability, and tech debt.

articles.title: Predicting AI job exposure
articles.summary: Many people would like to analyse which jobs, companies and industries are most exposed to AI, but this is mostly impossible without knowing how jobs will change.

articles.title: How will OpenAI compete?
articles.summary: OpenAI has some big questions about tech, user base, and what the plan is as incumbents match the tech and leverage product and distribution.

articles.title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
articles.summary: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features.

articles.title: Alibaba's Qwen3.7-Plus supports text, video and imagery inputs at low cost of $0.4/$1.6 per 1M token — but it's proprietary
articles.summary: Alibaba released Qwen3.7-Plus, boasting more multimodal capabilities and a 60% lower cost than the prior model.

articles.title: The Pulse: a trend of trying to cut back on AI spend within eng departments?
articles.summary: Top-down and bottom-up efforts to rationalize AI token spend, interesting AI coding stats from Cursor.

articles.title: Anthropic, Snowflake expand partnership to integrate Claude AI with enterprise data platforms
articles.summary: Anthropic and Snowflake expand partnership to integrate Claude AI with enterprise data platforms.
